title: Enhancing Consumer Confidence Through qPCR-Based Meat Authentication
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Consumer     trust   in   meat     products     is     a     key     factor   in   ensuring     public     health     and     sustaining     market     stability  .   Adulteration  ,   species     substitution  ,   and     fraudulent     labeling     undermine     confidence     and     can     lead     to     health  ,   economic  ,   and     ethical     issues  . Real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  )   is     a     powerful     molecular     tool     capable     of     detecting     specific     animal   DNA   with     high     sensitivity     and     accuracy  ,   even   in   processed     products  .   Implementing     qPCR-based     meat     authentication     helps     regulatory     agencies  ,   industry     stakeholders  ,   and     consumers     ensure     product     integrity     and     compliance     with     labeling     standards  .   This     approach     strengthens     traceability  ,   protects     religious     dietary     requirements   (e.g., Halal/  Kosher     assurance  ),   and     prevents     economic     losses     from     fraud  .   Scaling     up     qPCR     capacity     and     integrating     it     into     national     food     safety     programs     will     enhance     consumer     confidence     and     support     fair     trade     practices  .
